item.title: Why Ransomware is Stubbornly Sticking Around
item.description: In this edition of Studio 471, Jacqueline Burns Koven of Chainalysis discusses how ransomware is evolving and what challenges it poses for defenders.

item.title: Deck the Halls with Caution: Four Festive Cyber Threats to Look Out for This Season
item.description: As we wind down to the end of the year, Intel 471 details four critical cyber threats organizations need to be aware of to ensure they remain vigilant.

item.title: BlueSky: Understanding and Combating the Ransomware Threat
item.description: Intel 471 is a leading provider of threat intelligence and security research.

item.title: Four Benefits of Cyber Threat Intelligence - Kick-Start the New Year with Intel 471
item.description: Cyber Threat Intelligence provides benefits like early detection, vulnerability management, decision-making support, and more. Read on to discover how it gives organizations the advantage.

item.title: Cybercrime Exposed Podcast: The Xbox One Hack
item.description: Arman Sadri was a gaming hacker. But he didn’t recognize when he’d gone too deep. His life unraveled, and the FBI wasn’t far behind. His years-long dalliance on the edge led him to a place from which he’s still recovering.

item.title: Mitigate Supply Chain Risk with Cyber Threat Intelligence
item.description: Attackers increasingly are capitalizing on risks that come from supply chains. Cyber resilience is dependent not only on the strength of an organization’s own planning and defenses, but also that of its partners.

item.title: Why Behavioral Threat Hunting is the Big Thing for Cybersecurity in 2024
item.description: Intel 471 is a leading provider of threat intelligence and security research.

item.title: Testing the Efficacy of Security Software
item.description: In this edition of Studio 471, Simon Edwards of SE Labs walks through how his company tests security products based on the cyber kill chain and MITRE ATT&CK.

item.title: MonikerLink: Outlook's Achilles' Heel, Navigating the Perilous Waters of CVE-2024-21413
item.description: Intel 471 is a leading provider of threat intelligence and security research.

item.title: Cybercrime Exposed Podcast: Crypto Heist
item.description: In March 2022, North Korean hackers stole $600 million worth of virtual currency from the online game Axie Infinity. But blockchain investigators were hot on the trail.

item.title: Phobos Unleashed: Navigating the Maze of Ransomware's Ever-Evolving Threat
item.description: Active since 2019, Phobos Ransomware and its variants continue to target critical sectors through phishing and RDP attacks, prompting recent government advisories to bolster organizational defenses.

item.title: Speed and Insight: Intel 471’s Data Leak Blogs Enhancement
item.description: Your organization needs timely insight into the information these adversaries have released on their blogs more than ever before. With our latest data leak blog collection enhancement, you can take the preventative action needed to mitigate the impact on your assets.

item.title: How cybercriminals exploit the hospitality industry
item.description: In the second half of 2023, the hospitality industry was increasingly targeted by spam and social-engineering campaigns that led to malicious software.

item.title: Can Deepfakes Bypass Online ID Verifications?
item.description: Advances in generative artificial intelligence models are being used to create bogus identities, which will continue to pose challenges for ID verification.

item.title: Black Basta Ransomware and Threat Group
item.description: Financially motivated and Russian-speaking, Black Basta operates under the Ransomware-as-a-Service (RaaS) model and has targeted many countries worldwide; including the United States, Japan, Australia, The United Kingdom, Canada and New Zealand.

item.title: Intel 471 Drives Innovation at RSA Conference 2024 with Intelligence-Led Threat Hunting
item.description: That wraps up a phenomenally successful RSA Conference in San Francisco for Intel 471. The team built on our exciting acquisition of Cyborg Security and shared with customers how we’re supercharging threat hunting with the unrivaled breadth, depth, speed, and relevance of Intel 471’s cyber threat intelligence.

item.title: Introducing 471 Attack Surface Protection for intelligence-led business operations
item.description: Fast-paced cyber threats are driving more organizations towards cybersecurity solutions that help them anticipate attacks. Today, we unveiled 471 Attack Surface Protection, a powerful attack surface management solution that leverages Intel 471’s cyber threat intelligence to help customers quickly prioritize high risk exposures and improve their business operations.

item.title: What Can We Learn from Ransomware Attacks
item.description: In this Studio 471, Jamie MacColl of the Royal United Services Institute discusses a study of ransomware’s impacts from IT staff on the front line through to civil society.

item.title: BreachForums Saga Continues. What’s Next?
item.description: BreachForums, an infamous cybercriminal forum, is back in action after authorities disrupted it. Here's a look at the forum, including why it's back online and what may lie ahead.

item.title: Threat hunting case study: Looking for Volt Typhoon
item.description: Volt Typhoon is a state-sponsored threat actor group that establishes persistence in critical infrastructure. Here's how to perform intelligence-driven threat hunting to find possible signs of an attack.

item.title: Intel 471 Cyber Geopolitical Intelligence: Making the connection between geopolitics and cybersecurity threats
item.description: With Intel 471 Cyber Geopolitical Intelligence, government and organizational leaders can today begin incorporating geopolitical events into their cyber risk management and incident response plans to improve their decisions about cybersecurity investments, policies, and strategies.

item.title: Introducing the CTI Capability Maturity Model, a resource for measuring and building mature CTI programs
item.description: The CTI Capability Maturity Model (CTI-CMM) is an easy to use, vendor-neutral model that promotes a “stakeholder-first” approach to building a mature CTI program, evaluating its progress, and continuously improving it during the CTI maturity journey.

item.title: INC Ransomware
item.description: INC Ransomware is a malware variant that was first observed in July/August of 2023, and has since been a part of major disruptions mostly in North America and Europe.

item.title: Threat hunting case study: Uncovering FIN7
item.description: FIN7 is one of the most persistent and capable financially motivated threat actor groups. Here's how to threat hunt for signs of a FIN7 intrusion.

item.title: Are Telegram's New Policies Spooking Cybercriminals?
item.description: Telegram will now divulge IP addresses and phone numbers in response to valid legal requests. Some cybercriminals are planning to leave Telegram. We assess the impacts of this on visibility into the cybercriminal underground.

item.title: A Halloween Story: 10 Cyber Ghouls We Eyeballed In Q3 2024
item.description: In the third quarter of 2024, ransomware remained one of the most impactful threats to all sectors. Defenders were faced with a record number of vulnerabilities as nation-state actors, money lusting cybercriminals, and hacktivists with an ax to grind continued to make their marks on the digital threat landscape. Here are the spooktacular cyber threat intelligence (CTI) statistics.

item.title: Cybercrime Exposed Podcast: Raccoon Stealer
item.description: Information stealing malware is one of the most common ways that organizations end up infiltrated by malicious hackers. For several years, one type of infostealer called Raccoon Stealer ruled them all.

item.title: Holiday Season Cyber Threats (Part 2): Ransomware, Gift Cards, and Point-of-Sale breaches
item.description: The surge in online shopping and travel bookings during the holiday season offers rich pickings for cybercriminals. Black Friday, Cyber Monday, Christmas shopping, and increased travel throughout December offer ample opportunities for cybercriminals to profit from the high volume of transactions and the growing reliance on digital platforms in retail, travel and hospitality. Their objective is to compromise sensitive customer data, disrupt operations and erode trust, all with an eye toward monetization.

item.title: Collecting Useful CTI from Underground Markets
item.description: Extracting cyber threat intelligence on emerging threats and novel threat actors is challenging. Michele Campobasso completed his doctoral thesis in 2024 at Eindhoven University of Technology, and in Studio 471, he shares insights on these markets and maximizing CTI collection.

item.title: Bring Your Own Hunts to HUNTER
item.description: As a HUNTER customer utilizing the Hunt Management Module (HMM), you can now “bring your own” (BYO) threat hunting content to the HUNTER threat hunting platform.

item.title: Threat hunting case study: SocGholish
item.description: SocGholish is a malware campaign that spreads via hacked web pages. This is a guide for how to detect infections by searching in SIEMs and logging systems for attacker behaviors.

item.title: Threat hunting case study: RMM software
item.description: Attackers hijack or install their own remote monitoring and management software to penetrate deeper into organizations. This activity, however, can be detected using threat hunts based on threat intelligence.

item.title: Threat-hunting case study: Windows Management Instrumentation abuse
item.description: Attackers often use Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) for reconnaissance to map networks. This case study describes how to threat hunt for malicious use of WMI, which is also used legitimately by administrators.

item.title: SANS 2025 CTI Survey: It’s Business Time for Cyber Risk
item.description: The SANS Institute’s newly released SANS 2025 CTI Survey report reveals a major surge in use of cyber threat intelligence (CTI) by senior executive and business leaders to mitigate risk in strategic decisions, investments, and mergers and acquisitions.

item.title: A look at ‘Tinker,’ Black Basta’s phishing fixer, negotiator
item.description: The leader of the Black Basta ransomware group employed a trusted, experienced cybercrime actor nicknamed Tinker who he relied on for phishing content, call center management and negotiation skills.

item.title: mommy Access Broker
item.description: mommy Access Broker is enabling access-as-a-service operations through detailed intrusion guides and compromised credentials, and Intel 471 has released reporting and Hunt Packages to support threat hunting and detection.

item.title: Introducing Verity471: Cyber Threat Intelligence Ready to Operationalize
item.description: To shatter the cycle of reactive defense, Intel 471 is launching Verity471, the evolution of our trusted cyber intelligence platform, TITAN.

item.title: Bridging the CTI Gap: New Exposure Modules on Verity471 Deliver Market-Disrupting Views of Threats
item.description: Intel 471 is thrilled to announce the availability of our new Cyber Threat Exposure portfolio of solutions on Verity471, our game-changing CTI-powered SaaS platform, Verity471.

item.title: “Pig-Butchering” Scams: The Dark Side of Social Engineering and Why Terminology Matters
item.description: Intel 471 examines the rise of “Pig-butchering” scams, how they operate, and why the terminology used to describe them is sparking debate within the cybersecurity community.

item.title: NPM - Shai-Hulud Worm
item.description: Shai-Hulud demonstrates a highly effective and dangerous supply-chain attack methodology that threatens the integrity of the npm ecosystem and exposes downstream organizations to potential compromise.

item.title: In a digital age, US paper check fraud flourishes
item.description: Check fraud in the U.S. is booming. Fraudsters steal checks and sell them on underground channels where they are purchased, modified and deposited in hopes of a payment. Here's a briefing on this multi-layered crime.

item.title: How card fraud is powered by underground card checkers
item.description: Payment card "checkers" are used by criminal hackers to check the validity of stolen payment card details. Here's how this in-demand underground service works.

item.title: ClickFix: Tricking users into installing infostealers
item.description: Malware distribution campaigns that trick people into copying and pasting malicious commands, known as ClickFix, have been wildly successful. Here's an examination of ClickFix and how to defend against it.

item.title: Cybercrime Takedowns: Trust, Partnerships and Focus
item.description: Intel 471’s Public Sector team is regularly called upon to provide cyber investigation support, threat insights and real-life attribution in support of global law enforcement operations.

item.title: New FvncBot Android banking trojan targets Poland
item.description: Intel 471 discovered a new Android trojan, FvncBot, that masquerades as a security application for mBank, a major Polish bank. Our Malware Intelligence team analyzed its code, which is new and not based on other leaked malware code.

item.title: Hunting APTs: from state policy to TTPs
item.description: Our Geopolitical Intelligence and Threat Hunting teams collaborate closely to help customers monitor, respond to and mitigate cyber risks

item.title: Likely fake ransomware operator 0APT causes panic — Our analysis
item.description: A new threat actor has launched what appears to be a fake ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) operation called 0APT.

item.title: How AI and the human advantage beat tomorrow’s threats
item.description: Relying upon AI-driven cybersecurity to fend off cyberattacks raises a paradox: improving speed and productivity is a game changer, but it carries a risk to quality, fidelity and trust. The battle against tomorrow’s threats won’t be won by completely removing the “human element” but understanding where human judgement improves resilience.

item.title: CVE-2026-20127: Critical Cisco SD-WAN vulnerability exploited in wild
item.description: CVE-2026-20127 is an improper authentication vulnerability impacting Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Controller, formerly vSmart, and SD-WAN Manager, formerly vManage, components.

item.title: CVE-2025-68613: Zerobot botnet exploits critical vulnerability impacting n8n AI orchestration platform
item.description: Zerobot, a Mirai-based botnet known for targeting Internet of Things (IoT) devices, has leveraged a critical vulnerability tracked as CVE-2025-68613 to compromise instances of the n8n workflow automation platform.
